H1.prediction: No block structure possible. All 3 off-diagonal entries active.
H1.reason: With only 3 pairs, every channel is connected. The Steersman has no DOF to suppress.
H1.result: CONFIRMED — all pairs active, val loss identical to n=6

H2.prediction: 2 blocks of size 2 (one pair suppressed from each axis). Missing 1 of 3 RD axes.
H2.reason:
With 4 channels, the Steersman can form at most 2 pairs of size 2 
(using 2 of the 6 off-diagonal DOF, suppressing 4). This encodes 2 of the 3 RD coordinate 
axes. The 3rd axis requires channels that don't exist. This is the CRITICAL test: if n=4 
forms 2 blocks (not 3), it confirms the RD 3-axis geometry specifically, not just "some 
block structure."

H3.prediction: 3 blocks of size 2. Exact replication of C-001/exp3 results.
H3.reason: Replication at 10K steps. Should match C-001 at 4K with continued ratio growth.

H4.prediction_1: 4 blocks of size 2 (3 strong RD + 1 weak)
H4.reason:
The Steersman needs only 3 axes. With 8 channels, there are 5 "extra" 
channels beyond the minimum 6. Three scenarios: (1) the extra 2 channels form a 4th weak 
pair, (2) they get absorbed into existing blocks, (3) they remain disconnected. The result 
distinguishes "minimal 3-axis" from "fill all available structure."

H5.prediction_2: 3 blocks of size 4 (each axis gets 4 channels)
H5.reason:
12 = 2 × 6 channels. Most redundant parameterization. The result 
reveals whether the Steersman's attractor is "exactly 3 axes" (6+6 orphans) or "3 axes 
with proportional resources" (4+4+4) or "fill all pairs" (6 pairs).
